name: Day 21 — Bug Report Triage (GitHub Issue → AI Severity + Label + Slack)

nodes.name: GitHub Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes: Set this URL as your GitHub repo webhook. Event: Issues → opened, reopened

nodes.name: Filter — New Issues Only
nodes.notes: Only process newly opened issues, ignore edits/deletions

nodes.name: Extract Issue Data
nodes.notes: Parses all relevant fields from the GitHub webhook payload

values.content:
You are a senior software engineering lead responsible for triaging GitHub bug reports. Your job is to analyze each issue and return a structured JSON response.

Severity Levels:
- P0 (Critical): System down, data loss, security breach, affects all users
- P1 (High): Major feature broken, significant user impact, no workaround
- P2 (Medium): Feature partially broken, workaround exists, moderate impact
- P3 (Low): Minor bug, cosmetic issue, edge case, low user impact

Assignee Areas (suggest based on issue content):
- frontend: UI/UX bugs, CSS, React/Vue components, browser issues
- backend: API errors, database issues, server crashes, logic bugs
- devops: deployment, CI/CD, infrastructure, environment issues
- security: auth failures, data exposure, XSS, injection vulnerabilities
- mobile: iOS/Android specific bugs
- general: unclear or cross-cutting issues

Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no explanation:
{
  "severity": "P0|P1|P2|P3",
  "severity_reason": "One sentence explaining why",
  "assignee_area": "frontend|backend|devops|security|mobile|general",
  "assignee_reason": "One sentence explaining why",
  "suggested_labels": ["label1", "label2"],
  "summary": "One sentence plain-English summary of the bug",
  "needs_immediate_alert": true|false
}

nodes.name: AI Triage — GPT-4o-mini
nodes.notes: Uses GPT-4o-mini for cost-efficient triage. Swap to GPT-4o for higher accuracy.

nodes.name: Parse AI Triage Result
nodes.notes: Safely parses AI JSON output with fallback. Merges with issue data.

nodes.name: Add Labels to GitHub Issue
nodes.notes: Adds severity (P0-P3) and area labels. Create these labels in your repo first.

nodes.name: Assign Team on GitHub
nodes.notes: Assigns a team member based on area. Map assignee_area to real GitHub usernames in the Code node above.

parameters.body:
=## 🤖 Auto-Triage Report

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Severity** | `{{ $json.severity }}` |
| **Area** | `{{ $json.assignee_area }}` |
| **Summary** | {{ $json.summary }} |

**Severity Reason:** {{ $json.severity_reason }}

**Assignment Reason:** {{ $json.assignee_reason }}

---
*Triaged automatically by n8n AI Bug Triage Bot. If incorrect, update labels manually.*

nodes.name: Post Triage Comment on Issue
nodes.notes: Posts a formatted triage summary as a comment on the GitHub issue

nodes.name: Is P0 or P1?
nodes.notes: Only sends Slack alert for critical/high severity issues

nodes.name: Slack Alert — Critical Bug
nodes.notes: Sends a rich Slack alert for P0/P1 issues. Change #engineering-alerts to your channel.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'triaged', issue: $('Extract Issue Data').first().json.issue_number, severity: $('Parse AI Triage Result').first().json.severity }) }}

nodes.name: Webhook Response
nodes.notes: Returns triage result to GitHub webhook for confirmation
